objective: Maximize geomean decode speedup of a fused single-launch Kimi-Linear W4A16 megakernel vs baseline.py, correct within cosine 0.98

criteria.outcome: solution.py exposes a Model class with the same step(hidden, state) -> (hidden, state) interface and identical buffer/parameter names as reference.py, loading the reference weights unchanged.
criteria.observe: Read solution.py and reference.py; run check.py to confirm it loads reference weights and runs without interface errors.

criteria.outcome: Decode output (next-token hidden + updated state) matches reference.py by cosine similarity >= 0.98 across the checked seeds and context lengths.
criteria.observe: Run check.py and confirm all correctness cases report cosine >= 0.98.

criteria.outcome: The timed decode step is a SINGLE GPU kernel launch fusing the entire per-token forward (KDA+MLA layers, all int4 dequant-GEMVs, conv, KDA state update, MLA latent attention, MoE router+experts, RMSNorms, residuals), with a self-written fused int4 dequant-GEMV and no prebuilt quantized/model/attention/MoE kernels imported.
criteria.observe: Read solution.py to verify one launch and hand-written kernel; check imports for banned libs (bitsandbytes, torchao, marlin, gptq/awq, exllama, transformers, fla, flash_mla, vllm, sglang, reference.py, baseline.py); optionally profile to confirm single launch.

criteria.outcome: geomean decode speedup vs baseline.py > 1.0 (faster than baseline), geomean over context lengths 2048, 8192, 16384.
criteria.observe: Run benchmark.py and read the reported geomean peak_fraction/speedup; confirm > 1.0.
